response.justification: Candidate is a strong generalist software engineer with broad full-stack, web, and DLang experience, but the role requires native-level Japanese, deep C/UNIX systems work, Linux kernel modification experience, and embedded networking/security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching). The resume shows no Japanese proficiency, minimal recent C work (brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015), and no embedded firmware, switch chip, or network security background.
strengths: 20+ years of software development across diverse stacks and domains
Some prior C/C++ experience at National Instruments on a developer IDE
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing, open-source contributions
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency (hard requirement: native-level)
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or network security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, MAC auth)
No demonstrated Linux kernel source modification or chip-vendor SDK work
